# UC-18.2.5 · VMware NSX Transport Node Connectivity, GENEVE TEP Tunnel Health, and BFD Failure Detection

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Configuration, Fault, Inventory, Performance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch whether the invisible tunnels between your servers stay connected so applications can talk across machines without surprises. When the networking fabric squeezes packets too small or a tunnel drops during planned moves, we raise it early so teams fix the fabric—not blame the apps.*

---

## Description

Correlates VMware NSX transport-node posture across Policy inventory paths (`host-transport-nodes`, `edge-transport-nodes`) with operational REST snapshots (`/transport-nodes/{id}/status`, `/state`, `/tunnels`, `/remote-transport-node-status`), GENEVE TEP overlays on UDP 6081 (RFC 8926), BFD session states on tunnels (`UP`/`DOWN`/`DEGRADED`/`INIT`/`ADMIN_DOWN`), Transport Zone overlay versus VLAN-backed drift flags, MTU readings below the recommended GENEVE floor (~1700 bytes versus common 1500-byte fabrics), and Edge cluster failover fingerprints—surfacing east-west TEP blackholes and Edge transport degradation before workload dashboards stall.

## Value

Transport-node drift hides behind healthy ESXi pings: GENEVE tunnels may drop while kernels stay green, BFD timers declare tunnels down faster than syslog narratives arrive, and undersized MTUs silently wedgie overlays until workloads stall. Bringing Policy inventory, REST tunnel telemetry, syslog failover cues, and MTU hints into one Splunk workspace lets overlay engineers prioritize Transport Profiles, Edge clusters, and GENEVE underlays instead of guessing which hop broke.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt syslog feeds from NSX Manager, Edge appliances, and ESXi forwarders; layer timed REST or webhook-to-HEC collectors emitting vmware:nsxt:transport_node, vmware:nsxt:tunnel_status, and vmware:nsxt:edge_status JSON keyed by tn_uuid, tunnel identifiers, BFD timers (defaults near 1000 ms interval with three missed probes marking tunnels down), reported TEP MTU, and Transport Zone labels. Alert when risk_bucket stays in bfd_or_tunnel_down, mtu_below_geneve_floor, or failover_or_tunnel_syslog classes beyond suppression windows tied to CMDB maintenance.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) where **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing runs for **VMware NSX 4.2** (October 2024 GA), supported **VMware NSX 4.1**, or **NSX-T 3.2.x** estates aligned with Broadcom lifecycle statements.
- **Forwarder coverage** — Point Universal Forwarder tails toward **NSX Manager** `/var/log/syslog`, `/var/log/policy/policy.log`, `/var/log/manager/manager.log`, `/var/log/proton/proton.log`, **NSX Edge** `/var/log/syslog`, `/var/log/edgexb/*.log`, plus **ESXi** `vmkernel.log` and `/var/log/nsx-*.log` when diagnosing **host transport-node** faults rooted in kernel modules (**nsxa**, **vdl2**, **vsip**, **cfgagent**).
- **REST contracts** — Schedule HTTPS collectors or **HEC** writers covering **Policy API** host-transport-nodes, edge-clusters, edge-transport-nodes listings plus **Manager API** `/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/state`, `/status`, `/tunnels`, `/remote-transport-node-status`; authenticate with POST /api/session/create cookies or mutual TLS identities documented by VMware.
- **Parsing hygiene** — Normalize JSON keys (`transport_node_id`, `tunnel_status`, `bfd_session_state`, `reported_mtu`, `transport_zone_name`, `edge_cluster_id`) via props/transforms inside a **custom Splunk app** rather than rewriting TA **default/** without governance.
- **RBAC personas** — Bind **Auditor** versus **Network Operator** versus **Enterprise Admin** personas to collector credentials—least privilege must still expose **transport-node** reads required by Policy pulls.
- **Capacity guardrails** — Size indexes so four-hour stats workloads across thousands of **transport nodes** remain inside peer CPU/memory envelopes during **Edge cluster** failover storms.

**Step 1 — Wire ingestion and field contracts**
(1) **Syslog destinations** — Configure **NSX Manager** Admin → System → Settings → Manage Settings → Syslog Servers toward Splunk ingest over UDP 514, TCP 514, or TLS 6514; preserve **PRI** tokens per RFC 3164 / RFC 5424 so **`facility_hint`** aligns with **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing.
(2) **Sourcetypes** — Keep **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** TA-canonical; reserve **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`**, **`vmware:nsxt:tunnel_status`**, **`vmware:nsxt:edge_status`** JSON snapshots carrying **BFD**-aware tunnel enums (`UP`/`DOWN`/`DEGRADED`/`INIT`/`ADMIN_DOWN`) and **Edge** operational statuses (`UP`/`DOWN`/`DEGRADED`/`UNKNOWN`).
(3) **Alarm webhooks** — Map payloads into **HEC** so **`tn_uuid`** mirrors Policy inventory identifiers alongside syslog anecdotes.
(4) **Polling cadence** — Poll **REST** endpoints every 30–120 seconds—balanced versus **Manager API** limits yet responsive versus typical **BFD** timers on **GENEVE** overlays (**UDP port 6081**, RFC 8926, roughly fifty-byte overhead guiding MTU floors near **1700 bytes** versus legacy **1500-byte** fabrics).
(5) **Transport Zones** — Tag overlay **GENEVE** zones distinctly from VLAN-backed zones inside CMDB so **`tz_overlay_flag`** mismatches expose configuration drift rather than unexplained blanks.

**Step 2 — Build searches, macros, and correlation**
Paste the **`spl`** field into a saved search once indexes align. Factor **`eval risk_bucket`** logic into **`macros/nsxt_transport_risk`** so threshold edits propagate fleet-wide. Optionally join **`vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** (Splunkbase 1810) when **DRS** migrations—not WAN faults—explain transient tunnel churn.

```spl
(index=vmware_nsx OR index=vmware OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tunnel_status" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_status")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval syslog_pri=trim(coalesce(PRI, priority, syslog_pri, facility_pri, ""))
| eval facility_hint=case(
    match(_raw,"(?i)local[0-7]\\.[a-z]+") OR match(syslog_pri,"^[0-9]{1,3}$"), syslog_pri,
    match(_raw,"(?i)<[0-9]{1,5}>"), replace(_raw,".*<(?<lp>[0-9]{1,5})>.*","\\1"),
    null())
| eval tn_uuid=coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_id, entity_id, resource_id, id, uuid, "")
| eval remote_tn=coalesce(remote_transport_node_id, peer_transport_node_id, peer_id, neighbor_id, "")
| eval tunnel_state=upper(trim(coalesce(tunnel_status, bfd_tunnel_status, tunnelState, tunnel_health, "")))
| eval bfd_state=upper(trim(coalesce(bfd_session_state, bfd_status, bfdState, "")))
| eval runtime_status=upper(trim(coalesce(runtime_status, operational_status, status_runtime, status_api_status, "")))
| eval install_state=upper(trim(coalesce(install_state, preparation_state, tn_install_state, "")))
| eval config_state=upper(trim(coalesce(config_state, configuration_state, sync_state, "")))
| eval operational_state=upper(trim(coalesce(operational_state, combined_state, api_state, "")))
| eval tep_mtu_num=tonumber(coalesce(tep_mtu, reported_mtu, mtu_bytes, geneve_mtu, ""))
| eval tz_mark=upper(trim(coalesce(transport_zone_name, tz_name, tz_id, "")))
| eval tz_overlay_flag=case(
    match(_raw,"(?i)OVERLAY") OR match(lower(tz_mark),"(?i)overlay"), "OVERLAY",
    match(_raw,"(?i)VLAN") OR match(lower(tz_mark),"(?i)vlan"), "VLAN_BACKED",
    null())
| eval edge_cluster_mark=coalesce(edge_cluster_name, edge_cluster_id, cluster_name, nsx_cluster, "")
| eval risk_bucket=case(
    tunnel_state IN ("DOWN","ADMIN_DOWN") OR bfd_state="DOWN", "bfd_or_tunnel_down",
    tunnel_state IN ("DEGRADED","INIT"), "tunnel_degraded_or_init",
    runtime_status IN ("DOWN","DEGRADED","UNKNOWN"), "tn_runtime_abnormal",
    install_state IN ("INSTALL_FAILED","UNINSTALL_FAILED","UPGRADE_FAILED"), "tn_lifecycle_failed",
    config_state IN ("OUT_OF_SYNC","FAILED","PENDING"), "tn_config_not_synced",
    operational_state IN ("FAILED","ORPHANED","PARTIAL_SUCCESS") AND operational_state!="SUCCESS", "manager_api_state_bad",
    isnotnull(tep_mtu_num) AND tep_mtu_num>0 AND tep_mtu_num<1700, "mtu_below_geneve_floor",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(failover|standby|active.?switch|edge.?cluster.?member.?change|bfd.?timeout|tunnel.?down)"), "failover_or_tunnel_syslog",
    null())
| where isnotnull(risk_bucket)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(tunnel_state) as tunnel_state_latest,
       latest(bfd_state) as bfd_latest,
       latest(runtime_status) as runtime_latest,
       latest(config_state) as config_latest,
       latest(install_state) as install_latest,
       latest(tz_overlay_flag) as tz_class,
       latest(tep_mtu_num) as tep_mtu_observed,
       dc(remote_tn) as peer_dimension_count,
       values(edge_cluster_mark) as edge_cluster_refs,
       latest(facility_hint) as last_syslog_pri
    by tn_uuid risk_bucket
| eval tep_mtu_observed=round(tep_mtu_observed,0)
| sort -last_seen
| table tn_uuid risk_bucket tunnel_state_latest bfd_latest runtime_latest config_latest install_latest tz_class tep_mtu_observed peer_dimension_count edge_cluster_refs last_syslog_pri last_seen```

Macro hygiene — Version-control **`macros/nsxt_transport_risk`** beside savedsearches so auditors trace exactly which eval shipped quarter over quarter.

Evidence pairing — Capture **NSX Manager** Transport Zones / Transport Nodes screenshots keyed by **`tn_uuid`** whenever **`risk_bucket`** persists beyond one poll interval.

**Step 3 — Validate against VMware consoles**
(1) **Transport Zone parity** — Compare overlay versus VLAN-backed assignments against **`tz_class`** rows pulled by Splunk.
(2) **Tunnel drilldown** — Invoke **GET** `/api/v1/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/tunnels` manually when **`bfd_or_tunnel_down`** fires so **BFD** counters align with indexed JSON mirrors.
(3) **Edge failover narratives** — Review **Edge cluster** failover sequencing documented in VMware installation guidance whenever **`failover_or_tunnel_syslog`** spikes coincide with maintenance bridges—not every syslog burst implies dataplane loss.
(4) **MTU probes** — Run ping DF tests across **TEP vmknics**; **GENEVE** overhead explains **`mtu_below_geneve_floor`** alerts even when routing tables appear symmetrical elsewhere.
(5) **RBAC regression** — After credential rotations confirm accounts still satisfy **transport-node** **GET** routes before rewriting parsers.

**Step 4 — Operationalise alerting**
(1) **Suppress wisely** — Tie **`tn_uuid`** plus **`risk_bucket`** suppress windows to CMDB maintenance tokens covering rehearsed **Edge cluster** failovers.
(2) **Route deliberately** — Send **`bfd_or_tunnel_down`** pages to overlay SMEs; ship **`tn_lifecycle_failed`** incidents toward platform squads partnering with Broadcom support around **NSX Manager** healing procedures.
(3) **Evidence archives** — Export weekly CSV snapshots alongside dashboard PNGs referencing **`risk_bucket`** distributions for governance reviewers.
(4) **Dashboard boundaries** — Host north-south throughput visuals separately because this SPL emphasizes east-west **TEP** mesh posture rather than Tier-0 capacity charts alone.
(5) **HEC throughput** — Tune acknowledgement batch sizes during alarm floods so ingestion backlog never mimics tunnel recovery.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

• **Silent Splunk rows during outages** — Expand coalesce chains after fieldsummary exposes camelCase REST keys lacking FIELDALIAS coverage inside vmware:nsxt:transport_node streams.

• **Stale tunnel_state_latest** — Cron collectors stalled against Manager API rate limits; inspect splunkd.log on Heavy Forwarders running Python requests for HTTPS 429/401/502 fingerprints.

• **Missing facility_hint** — Intermediate relays stripped syslog PRI headers; enforce RFC-compliant templates or TCP syslog relays without rewriting angle brackets.

• **Oscillating bfd_latest** — Separate Transport Profile edits from WAN jitter using remote-transport-node-status snapshots taken minutes apart rather than seconds alone.

• **UNKNOWN runtime_latest near backups** — Edge VM appliances sometimes CPU-starve during snapshots despite benign ESXi CPU ready percentages elsewhere.

• **False mtu_below_geneve_floor** — Nested labs intentionally pin 1500-byte TEP vmknics; tag environment=lab before paging WAN SMEs.

• **Zero peer_dimension_count** — REST payloads omit remote_tn neighbours; enrich via lookups sourced nightly from GET `/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/tunnels` extracts.

• **Noisy failover_or_tunnel_syslog** — Correlate Edge cluster failover rehearsals logged in change queues before escalating fabric bridges.

• **tz_class collisions** — Duplicate Transport Zone labels spanning metros confuse lookups unless site_code prefixes normalize inventory feeds.

• **Geneve UDP 6081 blocked** — Firewall ACL denials coincide with tunnel_state INIT stalls—GENEVE requires UDP 6081 between TEP IPs even when ICMP succeeds.

• **Persistent manager_api_state_bad** — Suspect NSX Manager quorum faults documented by VMware/Broadcom—Splunk telemetry complements but cannot heal Raft outages alone.

• **Splunk_TA PRI drift** — Extend FIELDALIAS stanzas inside your custom overlay app rather than overwriting TA default silently.

• **Syslog EPS spikes sans tunnels** — Certificate rotation storms or verbose auditing inflate EPS without altering risk_bucket—rate-limit noisy priorities prior to paging overlays.



## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware_nsx OR index=vmware OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tunnel_status" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:edge_status")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval syslog_pri=trim(coalesce(PRI, priority, syslog_pri, facility_pri, ""))
| eval facility_hint=case(
    match(_raw,"(?i)local[0-7]\\.[a-z]+") OR match(syslog_pri,"^[0-9]{1,3}$"), syslog_pri,
    match(_raw,"(?i)<[0-9]{1,5}>"), replace(_raw,".*<(?<lp>[0-9]{1,5})>.*","\\1"),
    null())
| eval tn_uuid=coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_id, entity_id, resource_id, id, uuid, "")
| eval remote_tn=coalesce(remote_transport_node_id, peer_transport_node_id, peer_id, neighbor_id, "")
| eval tunnel_state=upper(trim(coalesce(tunnel_status, bfd_tunnel_status, tunnelState, tunnel_health, "")))
| eval bfd_state=upper(trim(coalesce(bfd_session_state, bfd_status, bfdState, "")))
| eval runtime_status=upper(trim(coalesce(runtime_status, operational_status, status_runtime, status_api_status, "")))
| eval install_state=upper(trim(coalesce(install_state, preparation_state, tn_install_state, "")))
| eval config_state=upper(trim(coalesce(config_state, configuration_state, sync_state, "")))
| eval operational_state=upper(trim(coalesce(operational_state, combined_state, api_state, "")))
| eval tep_mtu_num=tonumber(coalesce(tep_mtu, reported_mtu, mtu_bytes, geneve_mtu, ""))
| eval tz_mark=upper(trim(coalesce(transport_zone_name, tz_name, tz_id, "")))
| eval tz_overlay_flag=case(
    match(_raw,"(?i)OVERLAY") OR match(lower(tz_mark),"(?i)overlay"), "OVERLAY",
    match(_raw,"(?i)VLAN") OR match(lower(tz_mark),"(?i)vlan"), "VLAN_BACKED",
    null())
| eval edge_cluster_mark=coalesce(edge_cluster_name, edge_cluster_id, cluster_name, nsx_cluster, "")
| eval risk_bucket=case(
    tunnel_state IN ("DOWN","ADMIN_DOWN") OR bfd_state="DOWN", "bfd_or_tunnel_down",
    tunnel_state IN ("DEGRADED","INIT"), "tunnel_degraded_or_init",
    runtime_status IN ("DOWN","DEGRADED","UNKNOWN"), "tn_runtime_abnormal",
    install_state IN ("INSTALL_FAILED","UNINSTALL_FAILED","UPGRADE_FAILED"), "tn_lifecycle_failed",
    config_state IN ("OUT_OF_SYNC","FAILED","PENDING"), "tn_config_not_synced",
    operational_state IN ("FAILED","ORPHANED","PARTIAL_SUCCESS") AND operational_state!="SUCCESS", "manager_api_state_bad",
    isnotnull(tep_mtu_num) AND tep_mtu_num>0 AND tep_mtu_num<1700, "mtu_below_geneve_floor",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(failover|standby|active.?switch|edge.?cluster.?member.?change|bfd.?timeout|tunnel.?down)"), "failover_or_tunnel_syslog",
    null())
| where isnotnull(risk_bucket)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(tunnel_state) as tunnel_state_latest,
       latest(bfd_state) as bfd_latest,
       latest(runtime_status) as runtime_latest,
       latest(config_state) as config_latest,
       latest(install_state) as install_latest,
       latest(tz_overlay_flag) as tz_class,
       latest(tep_mtu_num) as tep_mtu_observed,
       dc(remote_tn) as peer_dimension_count,
       values(edge_cluster_mark) as edge_cluster_refs,
       latest(facility_hint) as last_syslog_pri
    by tn_uuid risk_bucket
| eval tep_mtu_observed=round(tep_mtu_observed,0)
| sort -last_seen
| table tn_uuid risk_bucket tunnel_state_latest bfd_latest runtime_latest config_latest install_latest tz_class tep_mtu_observed peer_dimension_count edge_cluster_refs last_syslog_pri last_seen
```

## Visualization

(1) Heatmap: risk_bucket versus tunnel_state_latest coloured by tn_uuid density. (2) Timeline: last_seen per tn_uuid annotated when bfd_latest changes. (3) Table: tn_uuid, tunnel_state_latest, bfd_latest, runtime_latest, config_latest, tep_mtu_observed, tz_class, edge_cluster_refs. (4) Single-value: percentage of tn_uuid rows absent from risk_bucket over trailing four hours after exclusions. (5) Optional geomap only when CMDB supplies Metro tags—otherwise facet by site_code.

## Known False Positives

**DRS evacuation bursts** — vMotion drains briefly collapse GENEVE adjacency until VMs settle on destination hosts; tunnel_state_latest may flash DOWN then UP without sustained packet loss once relocation completes. **Rolling upgrades** — Moving NSX-T 3.2.x toward VMware NSX 4.x restarts transport-node agents per host; runtime_latest may sit UNKNOWN briefly though sequencing honors VMware maintenance guidance. **BFD asymmetry during MTU sweeps** — Stretch-cluster engineers tuning paths from 1500-byte toward 1700-byte fabrics can observe INIT/DEGRADED BFD readings until timers reconverge across sites—suppress alerts aligned with WAN change bridges. **Edge rehearsal windows** — Planned Tier-0/Tier-1 failover drills purposely drive Edge TN DOWN states validating HA pairs—tie edge_cluster_refs to approved CRQs before paging overnight bridges. **Multi-TEP migrations** — Designs advertising alternate Tunnel Endpoint IPs intentionally ADMIN_DOWN stale peers until orchestration completes multi-home edits—Splunk sees churn though dataplane prefers healthy TEP pairs. **Stretched VLAN drills plus PMTUD churn** — WAN-layer discovery spikes tunnel_degraded_or_init counts despite overlays recovering seconds later across resilient paths.
